Any unexpected injury or incident that occurs at work or commuting to and from work is classified as a work accident. Occupational diseases/illnesses are those incidents that occur due to the scope of the work being carried out or due to the hazards present within the work environment.

Defined as a work accident or occupational event that causes the death of the worker.
Please enter the total number of work related fatalities for the given year:

A permanent total disability (Incapacidad total permanente) is the irreversible loss of the worker’s capacity to perform any gainful activity as a result of a work-related accident or occupational disease. The IHSS grants a lifetime pension equivalent to a percentage of the insured’s base wage according to the evaluation table. Reglamento de Incapacidad Temporal del IHSS, Art. 2.33.
Please enter the number of total permanent disability incidents for the given year.

A permanent partial disability (Incapacidad parcial permanente) is a definitive reduction in the worker’s physical or functional capacity, caused by loss or paralysis of a limb, organ, or function, resulting from an occupational accident or disease. The benefit is proportional to the degree of incapacity determined by the IHSS medical commission. Reglamento de Incapacidad Temporal del IHSS, Art. 2.32.
Please enter the number of total partial permanent disability incidents for the given year.

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

An occupational disease (Enfermedad profesional) is a pathological condition that occurs as a direct consequence of the work performed or exposure to physical, chemical, or biological agents characteristic of a given occupation or industrial process. Reglamento de Incapacidad Temporal del IHSS, Art. 2.16.
Please enter the total number of occupational disease cases recorded during the reporting year.

Employers must maintain updated records of work accidents, incidents, and occupational diseases, including causes, consequences, and days of incapacity for each case. Reglamento General de Medidas Preventivas de Accidentes de Trabajo y Enfermedades Profesionales (STSS-053-04), Art. 47(f)
Please enter the total number of days away from work.

A temporary disability (Incapacidad temporal) is the temporary inability of a worker to perform regular work due to a work-related accident or occupational disease. During this period, the IHSS provides medical care and a daily subsidy, until full recovery or reclassification as permanent incapacity.\n\nReglamento de Incapacidad Temporal del IHSS, Art. 2.29.
Please enter the total number of temporary disability cases recorded during the reporting year.

A dangerous occurrence or near miss (Suceso peligroso or Incidente) is an unplanned work-related event that did not cause injury or property damage but had the potential to do so—for example, a fall, explosion, chemical spill, or electrical failure. Reglamento General de Medidas Preventivas de Accidentes de Trabajo y Enfermedades Profesionales (STSS-053-04), Art. 42.
Please enter the total number of dangerous occurrences or near misses recorded during the reporting year.
